# Case Study Protocol

## 1. Study Objective

TBD

## 2. Case Definition

```markdown
Case:
Context:
Phenomenon under study:
Boundary of the case:
Timeframe:
Actors involved:
Software engineering setting:
```

## 3. Units Of Analysis

```markdown
Case design: Holistic single-case | Embedded single-case | Multiple-case
Case(s):
Unit(s) of analysis:
Rationale for each unit:
Comparison logic:
Selection strategy:
```

## 4. Research Questions

```markdown
Objective:
RQ1:
RQ2:
RQ3:
Expected contribution:
```

For explanatory studies:

```markdown
Hypothesis or proposition:
Evidence needed:
Potential rival explanations:
```

## 5. Theoretical Basis Or Analytical Lens

```markdown
Theoretical basis or analytical lens:
Relevant prior work:
Key constructs:
Expected relationships:
Scope of the theory or lens:
```

| RQ | Claim | Evidence source | Raw evidence reference | Analysis step | Rival explanation | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |

## 14. Ethical Considerations

Check:

- Informed consent
- Organization consent
- Confidentiality
- Sensitive company information
- Anonymization or pseudonymization
- Participant privacy
- Storage and access control
- Right to withdraw
- Ethics board or equivalent approval, when applicable
- Handling of prompts, traces, source code, logs, and proprietary artifacts

| Threat | Validity type | Why it matters | Countermeasure | Residual risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TBD | Construct | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| TBD | Internal | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| TBD | External | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| TBD | Reliability | TBD | TBD | TBD |

## 16. Reporting Plan

Default structure:

1. Introduction
2. Related Work
3. Methodology
4. Results
5. Discussion
6. Threats to Validity
7. Conclusion
